response:
Sec. 1  
Allergies to peanuts are immune system responses to proteins found in peanuts  
These responses can cause symptoms ranging from mild to severe  
Living underwater does not eliminate exposure to peanut proteins  
People with peanut allergies must avoid peanut proteins regardless of their environment  
Underwater living does not provide protection from allergens  

Sec. 2  
Allergic reactions occur when the immune system mistakenly identifies a substance as harmful  
Peanut proteins can be present in water if contaminated with peanut particles  
Avoiding allergens requires careful control of the environment  
Underwater habitats would still require measures to prevent allergen exposure  
Therefore people with peanut allergies cannot live underwater without proper precautions
